ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Na Abimelek faa nguan, nye anantwi, nye nkowaa, nye mfonabaa, na ɔdze maa Abraham, na ɔdze ne yer Sara sanee maa no.
translated: Abimelek spoke to him, and he heard, and he feared, and he bowed, and he said to Abraham, "Is your sister Sara with you?"

ref: More and more people were born, until finally they spread all over the earth. Some of their daughters were so beautiful that supernatural beings came down and married the ones they wanted.
text: Nyankopɔn ne mba hun nyimpa mbabaa no dɛ hɔn ho yɛ fɛw; na wɔwarwaar hɔn mu dza wɔpɛ nyinara.
translated: The chief and the people together perform the mbabaa dance to honor him; and the elders bestow honor so that they are pleased.

ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.
text: Na Laban frɛɛ no Jegar-sahaduta: na Jakob dze ɔfrɛɛ no Galeed.
translated: Laban freed them at Jegar-sahaduta; Jacob also freed them at Galilee.

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: Na aber a wi rokɔtɔ no, nda heii tɔɔ Abram do; na, hwɛ, sum kɛsenara na hu tɔɔ no do.
translated: And the people said to the elders, "Let us go to Abram"; and he said, "Let us build a house for them."

ref: and said to Ephron, “In front of these witnesses, I offer you the full price, so I can bury my wife. Please accept my offer.”
text: Na ɔkasaa kyerɛɛ Efron wɔ asaase no do nkorɔfo no hɔn asomu dɛ, Mbom sɛ ibetsie a, ɔno, meserɛ wo, tsie me: mebɛma haban no ne tsibo; gye no wɔ mo hɔ, ma minsie mo fun wɔ hɔ.
translated: And when Efron went to the earth, the chief was satisfied. Mbom said, "They will come, he is here, you are loved, come to me: my house and food; take and eat, but you will not see me and them again."

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Na wodzii ahyɛmu wɔ Beer-seba wiei no, Abimelek soɛree, nye ne safohen tsitsir Fikol, na wɔsanee ba Filistiafo asaase do.
translated: And they went to Beer-sheba. Abimelech gave them, and his wife and his daughter Phicol, and they gave all the land of the Philistines to them.

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: Na ɔsee bio dɛ, Nhyira nka EWURADZE, Sem ne Nyankopɔn; Na wɔmma Keenan nyɛ n’akowaa.
translated: He saw Nhyira, the son of Ewuradze, Sem and Nyankopɔn; Keenan is my father.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Nna akyɛdze no twaa mu dzii n’enyim: na ɔnoara dze, ɔtsenaa dɔm no mu anafua no. Jakob N’aperdzi Wɔ Peniel
translated: My son Jacob was in the womb with the mother: and he struggled, and prevailed over the thigh of the mother. Jacob wrestled with Peniel.

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
text: Soɛr, kɛma abofra no do, na fa wo nsa suo no mu; na mebɛyɛ no ɔman kɛse.
translated: Sir, may your right hand be strengthened, and may your country be exalted; and may the ruler of your people be blessed.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Na nsu no yirii wɔ asaase do nda ɔha eduonum.
translated: And the snake bit them on the earth, killing them.

ref: Abimelech called him in and said, “Rebekah must be your wife! Why did you say she is your sister?” “Because I thought someone would kill me,” Isaac answered.
text: Na Abimelek frɛɛ Isaak, na ɔsee dɛ, Hwɛ, ampaara wo yer a: na ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na ekaa dɛ, ɔyɛ m’akyerɛbaa yi? Na Isaak see no dɛ, Osiandɛ mesee dɛ, Bɛyɛ na mewu wɔ ne ntsi.
translated: Abimelek spoke to Isaac and said, "Where is your wound? Why did you do this? You didn't hide it from me?" Isaac replied, "The king's servants came, they asked me, and I said to them, 'You are with us.'"

ref: The servant had brought along an expensive gold ring and two large gold bracelets. When Rebekah had finished bringing the water, he gave her the ring for her nose and the bracelets for her arms.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, efumpɔnkɔ no wiee nsu nom no, nna banyin no faa sika mpɛtsea a n’ano tow dwetɛbona fa, nye ne nsakɔnadze ebien a n’ano tow sika dwetɛbona du dze hyɛɛ ne nsa;
translated: And he said, the elders said the people, and the young people gave money to buy other necessary things for, and the expenses which the other money is necessary to cover the country's expenses;

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Na wɔdze Josef kɔr Egypt, na Potifar, Farao no somfo a ɔyɛ n’abrafohen no, tɔɔ no wɔ Ismaelfo a wɔdze no kɔr hɔ no hɔn nsamu.
translated: And Joseph went to Egypt, and Potifar, Pharaoh's captain who was the chief of the guards, took him from the Israelites who had been brought with him to serve as a slave.

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.

translated: And Ewuradze's father gave Abraham this child.
